channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: A TikToker sues Roblox for using her viral Charli XCX dance without permission
item.description: If you thought you’d heard the last of the viral “Apple” dance, think again. The TikToker behind it is now suing Roblox over its unauthorized use.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: The US has a single rare earths mine. Chinese export limits are energizing a push for more
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — America’s only rare earths mine heard from anxious companies soon after China responded to President Donald Trump’s tariffs this month by limiting exports of those minerals used for military applications and in many high-tech devices.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New Jersey is suing Discord for allegedly violating child safety laws
item.description: New Jersey filed a lawsuit against Discord on Thursday, alleging that the social platform recklessly exposed children to “harassment, abuse, and sexual exploitation by predators who lurk on the platform.” The move makes it the first state to sue Discord.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Instagram launches ‘Blend’ to share personalized Reels with friends
item.description: When it comes to sharing Instagram Reels with friends, the process of three taps to get a Reel from A to B can feel surprisingly tedious. Now, Instagram has addressed that issue with its latest feature: Instagram Blend.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Google's digital ad network declared an illegal monopoly, joining its search engine in penalty box
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google has been branded an abusive monopolist by a federal judge for the second time in less than a year, this time for illegally exploiting some of its online marketing technology to boost the profits fueling an internet empire currently worth $1.8 trillion.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Netflix's first quarter builds on recent momentum as trade war drags down other tech companies
item.description: Netflix fared better than analysts anticipated during the first three months of the year, signaling the world’s largest video streaming service is still thriving as President Donald Trump’s policies cast a pall on the economy.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Why was Zoom knocked offline this week? There’s some finger-pointing
item.description: There’s a lot of finger-pointing going on between providers trying to explain this week’s two-hour outage of Zoom’s American service. On its website, Zoom says its zoom.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: IBM X-Force: Stealthy attacks on the rise, toolkits targeting AI emerge
item.description: Cybercriminals are adopting increasingly stealthy tactics for breaking into networks, while attacks targeting specific AI technologies are an emerging threat.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Lawmakers launch investigation into 23andMe after bankruptcy
item.description: A group of House lawmakers launched a probe Thursday into 23andMe's handling of customers' sensitive data following the company's filing for bankruptcy last month. In a letter to 23andMe, House Commerce and Energy Committee Chair Brett Guthrie (R-Ky.) and Reps. Gus Bilirakis (R-Fla.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Roblox issued subpoena for potentially exposing children to graphic, harmful content
item.description: The attorney general expressed concern over the ability of predatory adults to freely send messages to minors.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, NewsNation

item.title: Insta360 teaser suggests it could launch world's best 360 camera soon – here are 5 things I want to see
item.description: Insta360 has posted a teaser for a new launch event on April 22, and rumors are pointing towards a new X5. Here's what we want to see from the 360 camera.
item.dc:creator: mark.wilson@futurenet.com (Mark Wilson), TechRadar

item.title: The Sandman Season 2 Release Date Revealed - Netflix Original Coming to an End
item.description: The Sandman season 2 will be released in two installments in early July, according to Netflix. The fantasy show's final season will consist of six episodes in the first volume and five episodes in the second volume.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: Federal judge rules Google has illegal ad tech monopoly
item.description: A federal judge ruled Thursday that Google illegally acquired and maintained a monopoly over advertising technology, the second time in less than a year that the tech giant has been found in violation of antitrust laws. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Most Americans Skeptical of AI-Generated Information - A Survey
item.description: A Quinnipiac University poll reveals that three-quarters of Americans trust information generated by artificial intelligence only sometimes or hardly ever.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Best practices for migrating between public clouds
item.description: Historically, cloud migration usually meant moving on-premises workloads to a public cloud, like Amazon Web Services (AWS) or Microsoft Azure.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Samsung Galaxy Buds FE 2 earbuds specs spotted, revealing bigger battery and even Wi-Fi support
item.description: Samsung Galaxy Buds FE 2 filings spotted in the US and India, along with dual-band Wi-Fi specs and 'Buds Core' name.
item.dc:creator: becky.scarrott@futurenet.com (Becky Scarrott), TechRadar

item.title: Smartphones may boost children's mental health, study finds
item.description: The study's findings challenge the assumption that kids who own smartphones tend to fare worse than those who don't.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Shafer, NewsNation

item.title: Headlamp: A multicluster management UI for Kubernetes
item.description: As important as Kubernetes is to the modern cloud-native stack, it’s still not easy to use on your own hardware. That’s surprising, considering how long it’s been around.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Proposed rule change on endangered species triggers alarm for environmentalists
item.description: The Trump administration plans to rewrite part of the Endangered Species Act that prohibits harming the habitats of endangered and threatened species.
item.dc:creator: Tammy Webber, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 5 things you need to know before using AI tools in performing legal due diligence in the world of government contracts
item.description: Recent advances in AI are revolutionizing legal due diligence by enhancing efficiency and accuracy. The post 5 things you need to know before using AI tools in performing legal due diligence in the world of government contracts first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg wraps up testimony in antitrust case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg emphasized in federal court on Wednesday that he bought Instagram and WhatsApp because he saw value in the companies — not to take out competitors, as the Federal Trade Commission alleges in a historic antitrust trial.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Meta CEO Zuckerberg considered spinning off Instagram in 2018 over antitrust worries, email says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg once considered separating Instagram from its parent company due to worries about antitrust litigation, according to an email shown Tuesday on the second day of an antitrust trial alleging Meta illegally monopolized the social media market.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Visa cancellations sow panic for international students, with more than 1,000 fearing deportation
item.description: The speed and scope of the federal government’s efforts to terminate the legal status of international students have stunned colleges and universities across the country.
item.dc:creator: Annie Ma, Makiya Seminera And Christopher L. Keller, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: OpenAI GPT-4.1 models promise improved coding and instruction following
item.description: OpenAI has announced a new family of models, GPT-4.1, GPT-4.1 mini, and GPT-4.1 nano, which it says outperforms GPT-4o and GPT-4o mini “across the board.” In conjunction with the launch of the GPT-4.1 family, OpenAI also announced that it is deprecating GPT-4.5 Preview in the API.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: China pursuing 3 alleged US operatives over cyberattacks during Asian Games
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China said Tuesday it is pursuing three alleged U.S. operatives accused of carrying out cyberattacks on Chinese infrastructure during the Asian Games held in the city of Harbin in February. A notice from the Harbin police headquarters named them as Katheryn A. Wilson, Robert J.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Self-driving Tesla hits car, sign while driver sleeps behind wheel
item.description: According to police, the driver was asleep at the wheel and using Tesla's self-driving feature to navigate.
item.dc:creator: Drew Kolek, NewsNation

item.title: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg takes the stand in historic antitrust trial
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg took to the witness stand on the first day of a historic antitrust trial to defend his company against allegations it illegally monopolized the social media market.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nvidia lays out plans to build AI supercomputers in the US
item.description: There was mixed reaction from industry analysts Monday over an announcement from Nvidia that it plans to produce AI supercomputers entirely in the US for the first time.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Intel sells majority stake in Altera to Silver Lake investors in a deal valued at $8.75 billion
item.description: Intel announced Monday that it was selling the majority of its Altera chip business to technology investor Silver Lake in a deal valued at $8.75 billion.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Nvidia plans to manufacture AI chips in the US for the first time
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Nvidia announced Monday that it will produce its artificial intelligence supercomputers in the United States for the first time.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cisco SVP talks agentic AI, quantum security and sustainable infrastructure
item.description: When responsibility for a company’s long-term growth and differentiation in the market is part of your job description, it can be an adventure. Nathan Jokel, senior vice president of corporate strategy and alliances at Cisco, has stepped up to the challenge.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: NAVWAR using Catapult to find SBIR technologies faster
item.description: Shadi Azoum, the NAVWAR’s program manager for the SBIR/STTR programs, said about 60% of their entire SBIR and STTR awards take advantage of Catapult. The post NAVWAR using Catapult to find SBIR technologies faster first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Some top tech leaders have embraced Trump. That's created a political divide in Silicon Valley
item.description: SAN JOSE, Calif. (AP) — Like many in the tech industry, Jeremy Lyons used to think of himself as a relatively apolitical guy.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Via porn, gore and ultra-violence, extremist groups are sinking hooks online into the very young
item.description: PARIS (AP) — After his arrest, the boy's mother was stunned to discover that her 12-year-old had been learning how to kill and gorging on videos of decapitation and torture so gruesome they made even case-hardened French court officials look away.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, ABC 4
